name: Pickled Horseradish Sauce
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 2 half-pints
Procedure: The pungency of fresh horseradish fades within 1 to 2 months,
even when refrigerated. Therefore, make only small quantities at a time.
Wash horseradish roots thoroughly and peel off brown outer skin. Grate the
roots using a food processor or cut them into small cubes and pass through a
food grinder. Combine all ingredients in a large bowl. Pour the mixture into
sterile jars, leaving 1/4 inch of headspace. Seal the jars tightly and store
in the refrigerator.
